title: Ceres MW Scale Electrolyser Project Produces First Hydrogen
content:
Ceres Power has achieved a significant milestone with the first hydrogen production at its megawatt-scale solid oxide electrolyser cell (SOEC) demonstrator system, located at Shellâ€™s Technology Centre in Bangalore, India. Developed in collaboration with Shell since 2022, the demonstrator marks the first of its kind in India and signals progress in the commercialization of Ceresâ€™ high-efficiency SOEC technology for industrial applications. The system is capable of producing up to 600 kg of hydrogen per day at full capacity, with an electrolyser efficiency of 37 kWh/kg Hâ‚‚ â€” a notable performance improvement over conventional systems.

The SOEC unit features a metal-supported design that allows operation at lower temperatures, improving durability and reducing operational costs. This operational phase will help generate real-world data to refine the technology for commercial scaling. The development supports Indiaâ€™s broader ambition under the National Green Hydrogen Mission, which aims to build at least five million metric tons of green hydrogen production capacity annually by 2030. Leaders from both Ceres and Shell highlighted the projectâ€™s role in enabling the clean energy transition and developing economically viable hydrogen pathways for sectors such as green steel, ammonia, and synthetic fuels.
author: Analysts Account
